body: * render react docs to markdown * serve under `/.md`. e.g. for [/react/components/accordion](https://deploy-preview-1738--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/accordion) add [/react/components/accordion.md](https://deploy-preview-1738--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/accordion.md) * add a [/llms.txt](https://deploy-preview-1738--base-ui.netlify.app/llms.txt) * add a link to the markdown version on each page * currently the markdown is generated on the fly, right when the docs are built. We could also opt for pregenerating them and check them in our repo, this would stronger guarantee correctness in the absence of tests. How does the mdx to md transformation currently work: * parse mdx files into mdx ast * statically analyze the jsx parts and render reference tables and demos, extract out the description and title * convert those demos and reference tables jsx AST into md AST. * error on unrecognized jsx * run the mdast stringify plugin to obtain markdown Drawbacks * It only statically analyzes the JSX, this restricts a lot the valid jsx we are able to write * Introducing new syntax and jsx elements will be cumbersome The better alternative is to run after docs build is done and run over each html file and convert to markdown. This is currently not viable as not all demo information is available in the html. We can look into this after the new demo infrastructure is live. I'm adding two snapshot tests that serve as integration tests for the markdown generation Part of https://github.com/mui/mui-public/issues/423
title: [docs-infra] Create llms.txt
